sections.paragraph: m. dawn, morning (Arab. سُاحْرَةُ id.), Gen 19:15 Jos 6:15. Poet. there are ascribed to it eyebrows (see עַפְעַפַּיִם ); wings, Psa 139:9. בֶּן־שַׁחַר “son of the morning;” used of Lucifer [i.e. the morning star] (see הֵילֵל ), Isa 14:12. Hos 6:3, כְּשַׁחַר נָכֹון מֹצָאֹו “his going forth (is) established like the morning,” i.e. Jehovah’s advent, but Hos 10:15, “like the morning (quickly) shall he be cut off.” Adv. mane, at or in the morning. Psa 57:9 108:3 . Metaph. used of felicity again dawning after misery, Isa 8:20 (see אֲשֶׁר B, No. 8 ); Isai. 47:11 58:8 . The same figurative use is made of the word בֹּקֶר Job 11:17 נֶשֶׁף Jer 13:16 and Arab. صبح. Enwari Soheil: “in calamity there is hope, for the end of a dark night is the dawn.”
